scenes.text: Number 3.

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: The James Webb Space Telescope found galaxies that stopped forming stars more than 13 billion years ago.

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: These are not old galaxies winding down at the end of long lives. These are galaxies that burned through their entire gas supply in

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: the

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: first few hundred million years of cosmic history, formed all the stars they would ever form, and went dark.

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: The standard model of galaxy evolution describes a gradual process. Galaxies form stars over billions of years,

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: slowly converting gas into stellar populations. Some eventually exhaust their fuel or have it stripped away by interactions with

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: other galaxies, but this process takes time. A galaxy dying within the

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: first billion years of the universe is like a human reaching old age before their

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: first birthday. These dead galaxies are chemically rich, structurally mature, and completely inert.

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: They lived fast, and they died young. And they did it a time when the universe should have been nothing but a nursery.

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: The implication is that star in the early universe was not gradual. It was catastrophic,

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: orders of magnitude more violent than anything happening today. Galaxies were not gently assembling themselves.

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: They were detonating into existence and burning out. Number

metadata.segment_title: Galaxies that died before most galaxies were born

scenes.text: Number 2.

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: For nearly a decade, astronomers have measured the expansion rate of the universe using

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: two independent methods, and gotten

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: two incompatible answers. This discrepancy is called the Hubble and the James Webb Space Telescope

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: was supposed to resolve it. It did the opposite. The cosmic microwave background, the afterglow of the Big Bang,

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: mapped by the Planck satellite, predicts the universe should be expanding at 67 .4 kilometers per

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: second per megaparsec. A megaparsec is about 3 .26 million light years.

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: So this number tells you how much faster distant objects are flying away from us the out you look.

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: Measurements of supernovae and Cepheid variable stars, pulsating stars brighten and dim at predictable rates,

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: allowing astronomers to measure cosmic distances, consistently produce a value around 73 kilometers per

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: second per megaparsec. The gap between these numbers is roughly 9%.

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: The was that Hubble's measurements were slightly wrong. Distant stars crowded together, dust dimming the light,

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: subtle errors compounding over billions of light years. The James Webb Space Telescope has

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: four times the resolution of Hubble and 10 times the sensitivity. It observed the same Cepheid stars,

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: the same galaxies, the same supernovae, and the measurements matched Hubble's data almost exactly.

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: A value of 72 .6 kilometers per per megaparsec from Webb to 72 .8

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: from Hubble. The difference is under 2%. The measurement error is gone.

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: The tension remains. The universe is expanding at

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: one speed when you calculate forward from the Big Bang and a different speed when you measure it directly.

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: Both methods are verified. Both are precise and they disagree. Either the standard model cosmology

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: is missing a component, something like early dark energy, a mysterious force that pushes space apart,

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: giving the universe unexpected kick after the Big Bang, or the fabric of space -time behaves differently than Einstein's

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: theory of general relativity predicts. Either way, the math that governs the age, size,

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: and fate of the universe does not close. You are living in a universe that cannot agree with itself about how fast is

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: growing.

metadata.segment_title: The universe expands at two different speeds

scenes.text: eight to 11 billion years into the past. Dark matter is invisible. It does not emit light.

scenes.text: They tracked nearly 800 ,000 galaxies the constellation Sextans. The map reveals the cosmic web,

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: every cluster, every supercluster in existence. Think of it as the of the universe.

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: Galaxies form these filaments the way cities form along rivers. The map is twice as detailed as anything Hubble

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: produced, and the finest scales, it does not match the standard model. The Lambda -CDM model is the mathematical

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: model backbone of modern cosmology. It is the master equation that describes how the universe works,

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: from the Big Bang to today. It predicts how dark matter should be distributed. It predicts the density of filaments,

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: the sizes of voids, the clumpiness of the web at different epochs. This model has survived every test for over

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: two decades. It is the closest thing cosmology has to a constitution. The new map reveals smaller scale structures

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: and a degree of clumpiness that the model does not predict. The dark matter is not distributed the way the math says it should be.

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: Not at these resolutions. Not at these distances. The deviations are subtle, but they are real,

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: and they are present across billions of light years of cosmic history. Some theorists propose that dark matter interacts

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: with neutrinos, ghostly subatomic particles that pass through everything in ways the standard model does not account for.

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: Others suggest the model itself is an approximation that breaks down at finer scales, the way gravity works perfectly

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: until it not. This is not a galaxy that formed too early. This is not a star that is too old.

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: This the scaffolding of the universe itself behaving in a way that the foundational model cosmology did not predict.

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: The skeleton is wrong, and everything built on that skeleton, every galaxy, every void,

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: every prediction about the future of the cosmos, inherits that error. You are not looking at an anomaly.

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: You are looking at the floor shifting beneath the building. The universe not hostile. It is not malicious.

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: It is not trying to confuse you. It simply does not owe your models anything. It does not care what the equations predict.

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: It does not ask permission before violating a law that took a century to write. Every

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: one of these

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

metadata.segment_title: The cosmic web is wrong

scenes.text: fails. The telescope did not find answers. It found the edges of what we are allowed to know.
